ordinary|yes, expense remove was missing too. the reason is the same: i closed the first qa pass around the specific edit and payment-info complaints, but didn’t finish the full mistake-recovery checklist. that was a miss. i added it now: - each expense row has a delete icon button: `delete [expense title]`. - deleting removes the expense from trip state and recalculates balances/settlement immediately. - if the deleted expense was being edited, the form resets. changed: - [src/app.tsx](/users/edwardpham/documents/programming/projects/split-bill/src/app.tsx): delete handler and expense-row delete button. - [src/styles.css](/users/edwardpham/documents/programming/projects/split-bill/src/styles.css): danger icon styling. - [e2e/app.spec.ts](/users/edwardpham/documents/programming/projects/split-bill/e2e/app.spec.ts): regression test for removing an expense. verified fresh: - `pnpm test`: 11 passed. - `pnpm build`: passed. - `pnpm test:e2e`: 12 passed across desktop + mobile. - rendered browser check: deleted `airport taxi`, row disappeared, no console errors. ![delete expense proof](/tmp/split-bill-delete-expense.png)

principal engineer: keep recommendations realistic for the existing react/vite/typescript codebase and provide implementation-ready guidance. your job: redesign the full product, not just visual polish. improve usability, clarity, trust, mobile ergonomics, information hierarchy, workflow speed, and implementation quality while preserving all existing capabilities. project folder: `/users/edwardpham/documents/programming/projects/split-bill` screenshot folder: `/users/edwardpham/documents/programming/projects/split-bill/artifacts/redesign-screenshots` before making recommendations, inspect the existing project and screenshot evidence. start the project locally if needed: ```bash cd /users/edwardpham/documents/programming/projects/split-bill pnpm install pnpm dev ``` then review: - live app at `http://127.0.0.1:5173/` - `artifacts/redesign-screenshots/readme.md` - `artifacts/redesign-screenshots/manifest.json` - all png screenshots in `artifacts/redesign-screenshots` - source files under `src/` - especially: - `src/app.tsx` - `src/styles.css` - `src/i18n/translations.ts` - `src/app/sample-data.ts` - domain logic under `src/domain` - persistence/import-export logic under `src/persistence` product context: this is a responsive web app for splitting trip/group expenses. users manage trips, members, payment profiles, expenses, split methods, balances, settlements, payment qr info, sharing/export/import, language toggle, and qa notes. it targets desktop and mobile browsers, not native mobile apps. current state: this is an existing working react + vite + typescript codebase, not just screenshots. screenshots are captured from live app states. there are 58 screenshots covering 29 product states, each with desktop and mobile views. main product capabilities to preserve: - create/select trips - manage members - add/edit/archive members - store member payment details - upload payment qr image - add/edit/delete expenses - support split methods: - equal - exact amount - percentage - shares - support multiple payers - show formula preview - validate invalid expenses/member forms - show balances - show simplified settlement - show direct-payback settlement - mark settlement payment as paid - show transfer history - generate/display payment qr - export json - import json - export csv - print summary - copy share link - load shared trip from url payload - toggle english/vietnamese language - show qa/release notes and bug draft area - work on desktop and mobile brand state: no formal brand spec is locked. current ui uses inter/system sans, teal primary, coral action color, light gray background, white panels, 8px radius, and lucide icons. these are existing defaults, not mandatory. you may change visual direction, typography, color system, layout, density, navigation, and interaction model. important constraint: do not design a marketing landing page. the first screen should be usable product ui. design goals: - make the app feel trustworthy for money decisions. - make the main workflow faster: add expense -> verify split -> see who owes whom -> pay/share. - make mobile usable, not just responsive. - reduce visual clutter without hiding important financial detail. - improve clarity of settlement/payment information. - make sharing/export/import feel safe and understandable. - improve copy so users understand what will happen before they click. - preserve local-first privacy positioning. - keep implementation realistic for the existing frontend-only architecture. do the following: 1. audit current product from live app, screenshots, and code. cover: - main user jobs - primary workflows - current information architecture - current ux problems - visual hierarchy issues - mobile-specific issues - form usability issues - table/list usability issues - settlement/payment clarity issues - content/copy problems - empty/error/validation state problems - accessibility issues - engineering constraints and risky areas be specific. reference screenshot/state names from the screenshot folder when useful. 2. define product strategy. provide: - product positioning in one sentence - target user assumptions - primary use cases - most important user journeys - top 5 design principles - what should stay - what should change - what should be removed or de-emphasized - what should be future scope only 3. propose redesigned information architecture. include: - recommended desktop navigation model - recommended mobile navigation model - recommended screen/page structure - how trip selection should work - how member/payment info should be organized - how expense entry should be organized - how split methods should be explained - how balances/settlement/payment should be surfaced - how import/export/share should fit naturally - how qa/release functionality should be treated in product navigation 4. redesign each major area. areas: - trips & members - expenses & splits - balances & settlement - sharing & payment - qa & release - new trip / empty states - shared-link state - english/vietnamese localized state for each area, provide: - user goal - current problem - recommended layout - key components - interaction behavior - mobile behavior - content/copy changes - empty states - validation/error states - accessibility notes - engineering notes 5. create visual design direction. provide: - overall design tone - visual references or style keywords - color palette with roles, not just hex values - typography scale - spacing system - layout grid - card/panel rules - form field rules - button hierarchy - table/list design - financial amount styling - status styling - icon usage - qr/payment display treatment - mobile density rules - accessibility requirements avoid decorative-only ui. this is a practical finance/trip tool. 6. provide content design. rewrite or recommend: - app/product name if needed - main nav labels - screen titles - screen subtitles - primary button labels - secondary button labels - form labels - helper text - formula preview copy - validation messages - empty states - settlement/payment copy - sharing/privacy warning copy - import/export copy - print summary copy - qa/release copy - english copy first - vietnamese-ready guidance for localization keep copy concise, clear, and action-oriented. 7. provide engineering implementation plan. include: - recommended component architecture - suggested file/module structure - existing files likely to change - new components to create - css/design token approach - responsive implementation approach - state management approach within current app - how to preserve domain logic - migration steps in order - testing plan: - unit tests - e2e tests - responsive screenshot checks - accessibility checks - risks and mitigations - what not to refactor yet keep recommendations realistic for react + vite + typescript frontend-only app. 8. provide acceptance criteria. include: - product acceptance criteria - ux acceptance criteria - content acceptance criteria - visual design acceptance criteria - engineering acceptance criteria - mobile acceptance criteria - accessibility acceptance criteria - regression testing acceptance criteria 9. provide open questions. separate: - must-answer before implementation - can-answer during implementation - future product questions output format: use this structure: 1. executive summary 2. current product audit 3. product strategy 4. redesigned information architecture 5. ux redesign 6. visual system 7. screen-by-screen specification 8. content specification 9. engineering implementation plan 10. acceptance criteria 11. open questions constraints: - preserve existing feature coverage. - must work well on desktop and mobile. - first screen must be usable app ui, not a landing page. - do not invent backend/server features unless clearly marked as future scope. - do not hide financial details that users need for trust and verification. - do not rely on vague design advice. make recommendations specific enough for implementation. - if screenshots reveal bugs, layout overlap, poor hierarchy, unclear copy, weak mobile ergonomics, or accessibility issues, call them out directly. - be opinionated. if current structure is weak, propose a better one. do not stay close to the current ui unless it serves user workflows. ```
